# The Myth of Sisyphus

*[Camus](../philosophers/camus.md) · 1942*

An essay in which [Camus](../philosophers/camus.md) raises the question of suicide on the basis of [the absurd](../glossary/the-absurd.md), that divorce between the human demand for meaning and the silence of the world. Against the evasion of suicide and of the leap of faith alike, he defends a lucid revolt, of which Sisyphus eternally rolling his rock is the happy figure.
